careerProfile.title: Career Profile
careerProfile.subtitle: Mathematics & Statistics professional applying probability, finance, and analytics to decision-support systems and AI-assisted product development

professionalBackground.title: Professional Background
paragraphs: Daniel Cohen is a Mathematics and Statistics professional with an M.A. in Statistics & Applied Mathematics from Hunter College (GPA 3.93), MBA in Finance & Investments from Baruch College — Zicklin School of Business (Finance GPA 4.00), and B.A. in Mathematics & Economics from Queens College — Magna Cum Laude, Arthur D. Gayer Memorial Award in Economics. He passed Society of Actuaries exams P/1, FM/2, and MFE, grounding his work in probability, financial mathematics, and quantitative risk thinking.

His professional history spans college-level instruction at Kingsborough Community College, Queens College, Pace University, and Yeshiva University; Business Statistics lab teaching with Excel; and ongoing quantitative work at The Montfort Academy. Teaching strengthened his ability to communicate statistical ideas clearly — a skill that now supports analytics storytelling, dashboard design, and stakeholder-facing interpretation.

analyticsAiTransition.title: Analytics & AI Transition
paragraphs: Daniel is transitioning from mathematics, statistics, and finance instruction into analytics, AI evaluation, decision-support systems, and data-driven product development. The Daniel AI Suite — seven deployed Streamlit applications plus SQL and Excel workbooks — is deliberate proof of that transition, not a side project.

Portfolio optimization, hypothesis testing, Monte Carlo simulation, ML projections, betting EV, epidemic modeling, and AI evaluator workflows extend the same quantitative foundation built through actuarial study, business statistics labs, and graduate statistics training — now packaged as interactive applications recruiters can click through in minutes.

careerDirection.title: Career Direction
careerDirection.text: Daniel is targeting Data Analyst, Product Analyst, AI Evaluator, AI Trainer, BI Analyst, Research Analyst, Junior Data Scientist, Quantitative Analyst, and Financial Analyst roles. He offers credentialed statistics and finance training plus a deployed multi-app portfolio that proves independent product execution.

aboutMe.headline: Mathematics & Statistics Professional · Quantitative Analytics · AI-Assisted Product Development
aboutMe.subheadline: M.A. Statistics · MBA Finance · SOA Exams (P, FM, MFE) · Python · SQL · Excel · Streamlit · End-to-End Ownership
paragraphs: I am a Mathematics and Statistics professional who builds production analytics applications — not notebook demos. Graduate training in statistics and finance, actuarial exam foundation, and a seven-app product suite demonstrate how I turn quantitative methods into usable decision-support systems.

The Daniel AI Suite spans investment risk analytics, fantasy baseball decision engines, applied math labs, NBA playoff intelligence, music practice tooling, AI transition scenario modeling, and a cross-app Command Center. Each app is designed for real workflows: explore → analyze → decide → continue.
I use AI-assisted development to move faster while owning architecture, analytics design, UX, testing, and deployment end to end.

summary.headline: Mathematics & Statistics Professional — Analytics, Quant Modeling & AI Applications
paragraphs: M.A. Statistics & Applied Mathematics (Hunter, 3.93 GPA) · MBA Finance & Investments (Baruch, Finance GPA 4.00) · SOA Exams P, FM, MFE · B.A. Mathematics & Economics, Magna Cum Laude.

I design and ship end-to-end analytics products — seven interconnected Streamlit applications plus SQL/Excel workbooks — that turn probability, risk, and modeling into decision-ready tools recruiters can click through in minutes.
Target roles: Data Analyst · Product Analyst · Quantitative Analyst · Financial Analyst · AI Evaluator · AI Trainer · BI / Research Analyst.

repos.name: Investment Portfolio Analyzer
repos.oneLiner: Live-market portfolio analytics with health scoring, Monte Carlo, efficient frontier, and ETF overlap.

detailSections.title: Portfolio Health
detailSections.body: Composite health score across return, risk, diversification, objective alignment, and macro fit — with explicit strengths and failure modes for the current mix.

detailSections.title: Risk & Allocation Modeling
detailSections.body: Correlation, contribution, drawdown, efficient frontier, and Monte Carlo paths support allocation trade-off analysis.

detailSections.title: ETF Holdings Intelligence
detailSections.body: Holdings-level overlap and concentration views help users see hidden redundancy inside ETF sleeves.

detailSections.title: Product Design
detailSections.body: Beginner and Advanced modes share one analytics engine so education and deeper quant work stay consistent.

keyFeatures: Portfolio Health Score with plain-language what's working / not working
Live Yahoo Finance ingestion and refreshable holdings
Monte Carlo scenarios (P(loss), growth distributions)
Efficient frontier optimizer (max Sharpe / min volatility)
ETF Holdings Explorer for overlap and concentration
Beginner Mode coaching + Advanced analytics tabs
Applied Investment Insight → AMI handoff
detailHighlights: Health scoring that translates risk metrics into actionable coaching
Monte Carlo and efficient frontier on a shared analytics core
ETF overlap diagnostics beyond ticker-level pie charts

caseStudy.problem: Investors need accessible risk-adjusted analytics without institutional terminals or spreadsheet-only workflows.
caseStudy.data: Live equity/ETF prices via yfinance; user-defined holdings and benchmark returns.
caseStudy.analysis: Return/risk metrics, concentration flags, correlation structure, and health diagnostics.
caseStudy.modeling: Monte Carlo simulation, mean-variance optimization, and efficient frontier construction.
caseStudy.insights: Rule-based coaching and AMI handoff translate metrics into next questions and actions.
caseStudy.results: A live Streamlit product with dual UX modes and suite-integrated insight routing.

repos.purpose: Streamlit investment analytics product with beginner and advanced modes, live Yahoo Finance data, portfolio health diagnostics, Monte Carlo, efficient frontier optimization, ETF holdings overlap, and AMI insight handoff.

repos.businessValue: Makes institutional-style risk and allocation analysis usable for education and decision support without a Bloomberg terminal.
repos.recruiterTakeaway: End-to-end quant product: live data, analytics core separated from UI, dual-audience UX, and AI insight routing.
